summary: Comprehensive dark mode visual quality testing for Midnight Tech theme (iteration 275). All 10 test items tested. Dark mode works correctly for admin panel (sidebar + content), store page, hero sections, and table readability. Critical persistence issue found: dark mode does NOT persist after page refresh when aa_partner_code='AA' is in localStorage (which is the standard test environment setup) - because partner AA tenant has empty background_color so _applyBrandingToDOM never toggles .aa-dark. Dark mode DOES persist correctly when no partner code is set (platform tenant has dark colors saved).

ui_bugs.issue: Dark mode persistence fails when aa_partner_code='AA' is in localStorage. After saving Midnight Tech and refreshing, .aa-dark class is NOT restored because /api/website-settings?partner_code=AA returns empty background_color (AA partner tenant has no dark colors set). The .aa-dark toggle only fires when background_color is non-empty. Platform admin dark colors are saved to 'automate-accounts' tenant, not AA tenant. Without partner code: .aa-dark persists correctly. With partner_code=AA: .aa-dark=False after reload, --aa-bg=#f8fafc (reverts to light).

ui_bugs.component: Login page - email input not found
ui_bugs.issue: When testing /login without partner code (platform dark mode), the page shows the gateway (partner code entry page), not the actual login form. The email input is only accessible after entering a partner code. This is expected behavior but means the branded login form (with dark inputs) cannot be tested for the platform tenant alone. Minor issue: the .auth-input CSS class uses hardcoded background: rgba(255,255,255,0.96) which would show white in dark mode if not overridden by .aa-dark input selector. The .aa-dark input override does cover this: background-color: var(--aa-surface).

issues: Hero section has a blank/lighter area in the top-right corner (the blurred decorative orbs appear as slightly lighter patches against the dark background). This is subtle but visible. May be intentional for depth effect.
When loading with partner_code=AA, the store shows LIGHT theme (not dark) because AA tenant has no dark color settings. Dark mode only works for platform tenant (no partner code).

Section labels (PLATFORM, PEOPLE, SETTINGS etc.) use text-slate-400 which is overridden to color(srgb 0.545098 0.580392 0.619608 / 0.75) in dark mode. This is approximately rgba(139,148,158,0.75) - readable but on the faint side for uppercase section labels. Not critical.

test_results_detail.T1_midnight_tech_applies_immediately: PASS - Clicking Midnight Tech preset immediately applies dark colors: --aa-bg=#0a0f1a, --aa-card=#161b22, --aa-text=#e6edf3, .aa-dark=true
test_results_detail.T2_dark_class_on_html: PASS - document.documentElement.classList.contains('aa-dark') = true after Midnight Tech applied
test_results_detail.T3_admin_panel_full_dark: PASS - Both sidebar (background: var(--aa-card) = rgb(22,27,34)) and content panel (background: var(--aa-card) = rgb(22,27,34)) are dark. Screenshots confirm full dark admin panel.
test_results_detail.T4_hero_dot_pattern: PASS - .aa-grid-texture::before uses radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px ...) with 28px 28px background-size. Dot pattern (not grid lines) confirmed in CSS stylesheet rules. Hero element found with correct dimensions.
test_results_detail.T5_hero_text_readable: PASS - Admin hero badge text: rgb(255,255,255), Hero h1: rgb(255,255,255). Both use var(--aa-primary-fg) inline style which is #ffffff for dark primary.
test_results_detail.T6_users_table_readable: PASS - Table cells: color rgb(230,237,243) on transparent bg (dark surface shows through). Headers: color rgb(139,148,158) on bg rgb(28,35,51). No black-on-black cells (0/14 cells with contrast issue).
test_results_detail.T7_settings_saved: PASS - 'Settings saved' toast appeared after clicking Save Changes with Midnight Tech active.
test_results_detail.T8_persistence_without_partner_code: PASS - Without aa_partner_code in localStorage: after reload .aa-dark=true, --aa-bg=#0a0f1a (persists correctly).
test_results_detail.T8_persistence_with_partner_code: FAIL - With aa_partner_code='AA': after reload .aa-dark=false, --aa-bg=#f8fafc (light theme). Root cause: /api/website-settings?partner_code=AA returns empty background_color for AA tenant.
test_results_detail.T9_slate_pro_resets_light: PASS - Clicking Slate Pro: .aa-dark=false, --aa-bg=#f8fafc. Clean light theme restoration.
test_results_detail.T10_ocean_deep: PASS - Ocean Deep: .aa-dark=true (luminance of #071521 < 0.08), --aa-bg=#071521, accent=#06b6d4 (teal). Dark mode applies correctly.
test_results_detail.store_dark_mode: PASS (without partner code) - Store page: .aa-dark=true, product cards rgb(22,27,34) bg with rgb(230,237,243) text, search input dark bg with light text. Hero has aa-grid-texture. FAIL (with partner code AA) - store shows light theme.
test_results_detail.login_gateway_dark: PASS - Gateway page in dark mode: dark background, white text, partner code input styled dark.
test_results_detail.admin_sidebar_AND_content_dark: PASS - Both sidebar and content use var(--aa-card) CSS variable which resolves to #161b22 in Midnight Tech dark mode.

rca_persistence_bug.issue: Dark mode persistence fails when loading with partner code AA
rca_persistence_bug.root_cause: 1. Platform admin (tenant_id=null → DEFAULT_TENANT_ID='automate-accounts') saves Midnight Tech colors to 'automate-accounts' tenant settings. 2. When page loads with aa_partner_code='AA' in localStorage, WebsiteContext calls /api/website-settings?partner_code=AA which loads AA partner tenant's settings. 3. AA partner tenant has primary_color='#2563eb' but empty background_color. 4. In _applyBrandingToDOM, the .aa-dark toggle is inside 'if (s.background_color)' block - when background_color is empty, this block is skipped, so .aa-dark is never toggled. 5. Result: page reverts to light theme after reload when partner code AA is active.
rca_persistence_bug.reproduction: 1. Set localStorage.setItem('aa_partner_code', 'AA'). 2. Login as platform_super_admin. 3. Go to admin?tab=org-info. 4. Click Midnight Tech preset. 5. Verify .aa-dark=true. 6. Click Save Changes. 7. Reload page. 8. .aa-dark=false (light theme shown).
fix_options: Option A (Simple): In WebsiteContext _applyBrandingToDOM, always call _applyBrandingToDOM even if background_color is empty by using the DEFAULT_BG from CSS vars and checking against luminance. But this needs a fallback background_color.

Option B (Proper): When loading website-settings with partner_code, if background_color is empty, merge in the parent/platform tenant settings. Add a fallback query to DEFAULT_TENANT_ID settings when partner has no custom background_color.

Option C (UI Fix): In _applyBrandingToDOM, use document.documentElement.style.getPropertyValue('--aa-bg') as fallback if s.background_color is empty, then apply luminance check on that. This would preserve the last-applied state.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix dark mode persistence when aa_partner_code is set. The .aa-dark toggle is inside 'if (s.background_color)' block in _applyBrandingToDOM in WebsiteContext.tsx (line 423-432). When partner code tenant has empty background_color, dark mode is never toggled. Fix: either (a) fall back to platform tenant background_color when partner tenant has empty value, or (b) store the last applied dark-mode state in localStorage and restore on load regardless of partner code settings.

LOW: Login page input styling (auth-input class) uses hardcoded rgba(255,255,255,0.96) background. The .aa-dark input override covers most inputs but .auth-input class has its own hardcoded background. Verify that the .aa-dark input rule covers auth-input elements too (it does via 'input:not([type=color])' selector but confirm).
INFO: Hero section in store dark mode shows a blank/lighter area top-right from the blurred orb decorations. Consider reducing opacity of the orb divs in dark mode or making them subtler.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (HIGH): WebsiteContext.tsx _applyBrandingToDOM line 423: 'if (s.background_color) { ... document.documentElement.classList.add/remove("aa-dark"); }'. When partner tenant has empty background_color, the .aa-dark toggle is skipped. On page reload with partner code, dark mode is lost. Fix: add else clause to handle empty background_color case OR persist the aa-dark preference in localStorage separately.

DESIGN: The multi-tenant branding system means platform admin's dark theme only applies to the DEFAULT_TENANT_ID ('automate-accounts'). Partner tenants (like AA) need their own dark color settings. This may be intended, but for the testing scenario where aa_partner_code='AA' is set, the platform admin cannot apply dark theme that persists. Documentation should clarify this.

CODE QUALITY: OrgInfoSection.tsx theme preset buttons still use hardcoded Tailwind classes (bg-white, border-slate-200, hover:bg-slate-50) instead of CSS variables. These are overridden by .aa-dark CSS rules, but it would be cleaner to use CSS variables directly. Not a bug since the .aa-dark overrides work.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (8/10 test items passing, 2 failing: persistence with partner code, login dark mode form not testable)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Midnight Tech dark theme (iteration 275). Key finding: dark mode works correctly LIVE (clicking preset applies instantly) and PERSISTS when no partner code is set. Fails to persist when aa_partner_code='AA' because AA tenant has empty background_color. Test setup: to test dark mode persistence properly, do NOT set aa_partner_code in localStorage (or configure AA tenant with same dark colors). Auth: admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123!. CSS: .aa-dark class on html element is the key. aa-grid-texture uses radial-gradient (dot pattern). Visual quality confirmed good from screenshots: dark sidebar, dark content, readable table text (rgb(230,237,243) on dark bg), no black-on-black. Hero dot pattern confirmed in CSS.
